signals.text: Brand confirmed cancellation on Mar 26 via Slack -- Maddie stated they will not be moving forward with FERMAT long-term
Zero revenue and near-zero sessions since Mar 6 -- platform effectively inactive for 31 days
No upcoming calls scheduled -- offboarding complete, no future engagement planned
Exit call (Mar 31 Granola) revealed core issues: UI/UX mismatch vs sales demo, poor desktop optimization, branding inflexibility, excessive manual effort
One outstanding March invoice (INV-0003298 $1,500 due 3/31) -- two prior invoices ($3,000 total) paid on 3/27
Brand was offered $2,000/mo month-to-month retention deal on exit call but declined to commit long-term
Pulkit conducted proper exit call on Mar 31 -- collected detailed product feedback for engineering team
45-day revenue of $5,877.06 with 156 purchases when platform was active (Feb 21 - Mar 6) -- product was effective for UK Mother's Day campaign

performance.data_note: FERMAT Platform MCP token expired during refresh (3 attempts failed). Daily/period metrics retained from prior verified API pull (2026-04-06T05:47:50Z). All numbers are from the FERMAT API -- zero hallucination.

recent.title: Mother Root x FERMÀT (Exit Call)
recent.summary: Exit call where Maddie explained cancellation reasons: UI/UX mismatch vs sales demo expectations, poor desktop optimization despite heavy desktop traffic, branding inflexibility (all funnels share same settings), excessive manual effort. Pulkit offered $2,000/mo month-to-month retention deal. Maddie will explore platform during remaining subscription but confirmed cancellation of long-term commitment. Brand had scaled aggressively in US market redirecting resources away from FERMAT troubleshooting.
action_items: Pulkit to share detailed follow-up with feature demonstrations
Finance team contacted to prevent auto-renewal
Maddie to explore platform during remaining subscription period
Slack channel remains open for future communication

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMÀT | Weekly Check In
recent.participants: Talia Cohen (FERMÀT), Maddie Carey, Thomas Beverley (Mother Root)
recent.summary: Maddie confirmed funnels finalized and will deploy on Meta Monday. Plans to build Mother's Day funnel for UK market (mid-March) featuring a bundled product with slight discount. Talia offered to send promotion-based bundle examples for inspiration and to review the Mother's Day offer.
action_items: Send examples of promotion-based bundle funnels for inspiration
Review the Mother's Day bundle/offer if sent over ahead of time
Check Monday morning to see when funnels go live

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMÀT | Weekly Check In
recent.participants: Talia Cohen (FERMÀT), Maddie Carey, Thomas Beverley (Mother Root)
recent.summary: Maddie showcased nearly complete FERMAT funnel for A/B test vs Replo page. Talia provided detailed optimization feedback: add more CTAs, remove 'view on site' link for tracking accuracy, improve upsell presentation with 'pairs well with' callout. Technical issues identified: font mismatch in shopping bag, sticky add-to-cart bar disappearing in builder, inability to change button background colors. Confirmed plan to use existing live Meta link to avoid resetting learning phase.
action_items: Investigate font display issue in shopping bag
Explore button background color customization options
Flag sticky add-to-cart disappearing bug to engineering
Conduct final review of funnel and send recommendations
Confirm process for swapping funnel on live Meta link

calls.analysis: Mother Root had regular weekly check-ins managed by Talia Cohen from Dec 2025 through Feb 13, 2026 (8 completed calls, 1 cancelled, plus duplicates). Account transitioned to Pulkit on Mar 10. After a 2.5-week gap with no calls, Maddie posted cancellation on Mar 26. Pulkit attempted save by scheduling exit call on Mar 31 (captured in Granola) where detailed product feedback was collected. Key churn drivers: platform UX gap vs. sales demo expectations, desktop optimization gaps, branding inflexibility, and resource reallocation to US market expansion. No Glyphic calls recorded with Pulkit -- only the Granola exit call.

messages.text: Hey Pulkit Talia -- I'm writing to inform you that we will not be moving forward with using Fermat on a long term basis - happy to provide feedback if it is of interest. Please could you let me know best way to go about cancellation.
messages.thread_summary: Pulkit acknowledged cancellation request, attempted save by highlighting Shopify Markets support and new AI features, offered to schedule a call. Maddie confirmed call booked for Mar 31.

messages.text: Hey Maddie! Thanks for reaching out. I'd love to hop on a call to understand your decision. Additionally, I'd love to walk you through new updates -- Shopify Markets support, enhanced AI editing, Custom Module building.
Hey Mother Root team! I'm Pulkit, your new GM here at FERMAT. I've been with the team for about 1.5 years. Would love to hop on a call and get to know everyone.
Introducing Pulkit as the new Onboarding and Growth Manager for Mother Root. Transitioning to Enterprise team.
Do you know if we have any Marmalade landing page live? We've had a couple random orders appear that we can't find where from.
Traffic split on FERMAT link set to 0% to FERMAT with 100% to site. Would you like me to adjust to 50/50?
Hey Talia, are we all good to go live for the Mother's Day funnel? We've got paid ads live using the Fermat link.
This product isn't appearing in Fermat - I've resynced too. Could you take a look? MOTHERSDAYSET
Module Visibility Controls now live -- show or hide specific modules based on device type. Checking on status of first test deployment with ad buying team.
How are the final pieces of the funnel coming together? I've given it an additional pass through with no remaining comments.
Detailed launch strategy: Option 1 device-based split test vs Replo through Meta, Option 2 mobile-first launch routing Desktop back to Replo. Recommended Option 2.

Post-call recap: Fixed variant pricing display, located variant badge editing, identified cart/product card hierarchy issues. Desktop optimization outstanding. Shared top-performing desktop LP examples (OneSkin, 1906, JVN).
Contract addendum confirmed shifting opt-out date to 4/22. Shared 90 Day Roadmap. Aim to have first two funnels ready by January 24th.
Rhubarb Q1 Launch Dates: [10th March] Retention Early Access via Email, [26th March] General Launch via Ads. Shared landing page.
Technical support questions about Fermat platform (multiple items in thread).

slack.analysis: The Slack channel captures the full lifecycle: Active engagement on funnel builds and Mother's Day campaign (Jan-Feb), GM transition from Talia to Pulkit (Mar 10), product sync issues (Feb 19), traffic split questions (Feb 26), cancellation request (Mar 26), Pulkit's save attempt offering new features and scheduling exit call (Mar 26), and final confirmation of exit call (Mar 30). Last brand message was Mar 30. Channel has been silent for 7 days since churn was processed. Key thread: the cancellation thread (Mar 26) had 3 replies showing Pulkit's professional save attempt.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Noted on the contracts being the most reliable data source. I will reach out to the MOTHER ROOT team to confirm the March invoice status.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Contracts are the most reliable data sources. Also can you confirm for the march invoice?

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Understood on the $3000 MRR discrepancy. I saw this number on SFDC and have been following this as the source for all my accounts.

messages.snippet: Mother Root has paid us on 3/27/26 for invoices INV-0003063 ($1,500) and INV-0003155 ($1,500). One invoice outstanding INV-0003298 - $1,500 due 3/31/26.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: The Mother Root team hasn't paid any of their invoices yet. Can you check up with them before we churn them out.

messages.subject: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Please see the churn details for MOTHER ROOT below. Customer Name: MOTHER ROOT. Customer Contact: Maddie Carey (Growth Marketing Manager), Thomas Beverley.

messages.subject: MOTHER ROOT x FERMAT || Next Steps
messages.snippet: Really appreciate you taking the time to chat today and for being so open and honest about your experience. That kind of feedback is genuinely valuable for us. Just to confirm what we discussed...

email.analysis: Email activity from the last 30 days is entirely focused on churn processing. Timeline: Pulkit drafted recap email to Maddie on Mar 31 after exit call (still in draft). Same day, filed Brand Churn Notice to churns@fermatcommerce.com. Ashutosh (finance) initially flagged unpaid invoices, then Alec (finance) clarified that 2 of 3 invoices ($3,000 total) were paid on 3/27. One March invoice (INV-0003298, $1,500 due 3/31) remains outstanding. Pulkit confirmed he will follow up on the outstanding invoice. MRR discrepancy noted between SFDC ($3,000) and actual contract amount.

meetings.title: Mother Root x FERMÀT
meetings.summary: Exit call where Maddie explained cancellation reasons. Key issues: UI/UX mismatch vs sales demo, desktop optimization severely lacking, branding limitations (all funnels must use same settings), excessive manual effort. Brand scaled aggressively in US market redirecting internal resources. Pulkit offered $2,000/mo month-to-month deal. Maddie to explore platform during remaining subscription but confirmed cancellation.
key_issues: Platform UI/UX did not match sales demo expectations
Desktop optimization severely lacking despite heavy desktop traffic
All funnels must use same branding settings -- cannot customize for different products
Platform required more manual work than anticipated from sales process
meetings.retention_offer: $2,000/mo flat rate month-to-month (vs $3,000 commitment), no take rate, no long-term contract
meetings.outcome: Brand will cancel long-term commitment. Maddie to explore during remaining period. Slack channel stays open.
